queries.title: Local Store Inventory Recommendations
queries.description: Find products a customer viewed online that are currently in-stock at their preferred physical store, driving local foot traffic.
queries.gql:
GRAPH RetailMarketingGraph
MATCH (c:Customer {customer_id: 'cust-0000000'})-[:PrefersStore]->(s:Store),
      (c)-[:Views]->(p:Product)-[inv:Inventory]->(s)
WHERE inv.quantity > 0
RETURN p.name AS viewed_product, s.name AS local_store, s.city AS city, inv.quantity AS stock_count, inv.aisle_location AS aisle
LIMIT 5
queries.talk_track: This is the holy grail of omnichannel retail. We instantly correlate digital browsing history (Views) with physical store preferences (PrefersStore) and real-time inventory levels (Inventory) in a single declarative MATCH query. With Spanner Graph, you can trigger real-time, hyper-local push notifications like 'The item you viewed online is in-stock on Aisle 4 at your local physical store!' with sub-second latency, driving immediate foot traffic and conversion.

queries.title: Real-Time Collaborative Recommendations
queries.description: Expose cross-customer buying patterns to recommend products purchased by other customers who bought the same items (Customers who bought X also bought Y).
queries.gql:
GRAPH RetailMarketingGraph
MATCH (c:Customer {customer_id: 'cust-0000000'})-[:Purchased]->(p:Product)<-[:Purchased]-(other:Customer)-[:Purchased]->(rec:Product)
WHERE rec.product_id != p.product_id
  AND NOT EXISTS { MATCH (c)-[:Purchased]->(rec) }
RETURN DISTINCT rec.name AS recommended_product, rec.category AS category, rec.price AS price
LIMIT 5
queries.talk_track: Personalized recommendation engines are typically powered by slow batch ETL pipelines. Spanner Graph allows you to execute real-time collaborative filtering directly on your live transactional database. By traversing the customer-product-customer-product path, we recommend highly relevant items at the exact moment of checkout, maximizing average order value without any processing delays.

queries.title: High-Value Loyalty Segment Affinity
queries.description: Trace cross-selling affinity for Platinum loyalty members who viewed premium products in a target category but purchased items in other categories.
queries.gql:
GRAPH RetailMarketingGraph
MATCH (c:Customer {loyalty_tier: 'Platinum'})-[:Views]->(p1:Product {category: 'Electronics'})-[]->(),
      (c)-[:Purchased]->(p2:Product)
WHERE p2.category != 'Electronics' AND p2.price > 100
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS customer_name, p2.name AS purchased_product, p2.category AS category, p2.price AS price
LIMIT 5
queries.talk_track: Understanding segment affinity helps marketing teams design precise cross-selling campaigns. This query traces our most valuable customers (Platinum) who show digital intent in a premium category (Electronics) and correlates it with their actual purchases in other categories. Executing this on a live transaction graph allows you to adjust homepage banners and offers dynamically based on real-time buying patterns.
